name: Pad Thai Pseudo-Vegetarian Style
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Thai rice noodles (about 1/4 inch thick)
3 Green onions (sliced on the diagonal, including white part)
recipeInstructions:
1. Take some dried red chili peppers and cook them in an ungreased wok over low heat, stirring constantly until they start to brown. Grind in a coffee grinder or spice mill.
2. Combine the first four ingredients (fish sauce, vinegar, sugar) and let sit until the sugar dissolves.
3. Soak rice noodles/sticks in warm water until they are soft but don't disintegrate when pressed, about 40 minutes. Drain.
4. Drain tofu by wrapping it in a clean towel and pressing with a large can of tomatoes, about 30 minutes. Cut into 1/4 inch cubes.
5. Rinse dried shrimp in water and drain.
6. Put oil in the wok, heat to medium, add crushed garlic. Swirl around for about a minute.
7. Add noodles and mix around for a minute or two.
8. Add tofu and shrimp, then immediately add the liquid mixture from step 1. Keep stirring until all the liquid has been absorbed into the noodles, about a couple of minutes.
9. Lower heat to low or medium-low. Push some of the ingredients aside at one end of the wok, break an egg in, and cover with noodles. Do the same at the other end of the wok. Let the eggs cook undisturbed until the yolks are practically cooked, about three minutes.
10. Slip a slotted spoon under each egg in turn, bringing them upward through the noodles while shaking to break up the cooked egg into tiny bits.
11. Mix in sprouts and green onions. Cook for another two minutes.
12. Turn off heat. Add crushed peanuts and enough roasted red chilies to your desired level of hotness.
